title: Supervisors & Council Members, 2004–2026
intro: Who has held Riverhead's top elected offices over roughly the last two decades — sourced from RiverheadLOCAL, Riverhead News-Review, and campaign/election records.
scopeNote: This is not a complete roster back to the Town's 1792 founding — the town's own Historian's office cites Daniel Well as its first Supervisor, elected at the town's first meeting in April 1792, and Sylvester Miller as its longest-serving (1841–1861), but a full year-by-year record isn't published online. This page covers what could be sourced for the last ~20 years. The Town Board always has a Supervisor plus four council seats; coverage is complete for every seat from 2016 onward, but thinner for 2008–2016, when not every seat-holder could be pinned down with a specific source. If you can source a correction or fill a gap, please flag it.

supervisors.name: Phil Cardinale
supervisors.note: Defeated by Sean Walter in the November 2009 election.
sources: Riverhead News-Review, “Former Riverhead supervisor Phil Cardinale hints at state run” (March 2012).

supervisors.name: Sean Walter
supervisors.note: Elected four times, serving 2010–2017; defeated by Laura Jens-Smith in the November 2017 election. Later elected Riverhead Town Justice in 2020.
sources: Riverhead News-Review, “Q&A with outgoing Riverhead Town supervisor Sean Walter” (Dec. 2017).
RiverheadLOCAL, “Sean Walter sworn in as Riverhead town justice” (Nov. 2020).

supervisors.name: Laura Jens-Smith
supervisors.note: The first woman elected Riverhead Town Supervisor in the town's 227-year history at the time. Defeated by Yvette Aguiar in the November 2019 election.
sources: RiverheadLOCAL, “History is made in Riverhead: Jens-Smith sworn in as first woman town supervisor” (Jan. 1, 2018).

supervisors.name: Yvette Aguiar
supervisors.note: The Town's 63rd Supervisor. Defeated incumbent Laura Jens-Smith in November 2019, defeated a challenge from councilwoman Catherine Kent in 2021, and did not seek a third term — her time in office ended December 31, 2023.
sources: RiverheadLOCAL, “Riverhead supervisor bows out: Aguiar announces she will not seek a third term of office” (Feb. 2023).
Riverhead News-Review, “Outgoing Riverhead Town Supervisor Yvette Aguiar reflects on her tenure” (Dec. 2023).

supervisors.name: Tim Hubbard
supervisors.note: The Town's 64th Supervisor. Previously an 8-year Town Councilman (2016–2023). Lost the November 2025 election to Jerry Halpin by 37 votes after a recount.
sources: RiverheadLOCAL, “A new day in Riverhead: Tim Hubbard… sworn in as the county seat's 64th town supervisor” (Jan. 2, 2024).
East End Beacon, “With Recount Complete, Riverhead Town Supervisor Tim Hubbard Concedes.”

supervisors.name: Jerome Halpin
supervisors.note: Currently serving. A pastor of North Shore Christian Church for about 22 years and a political newcomer before winning the Supervisor race by 37 votes.
sources: RiverheadLOCAL, “Pastor Jerry Halpin will be sworn in as town supervisor” (Dec. 31, 2025).

councilMembers.name: James M. Wooten
councilMembers.note: Elected in November 2007. Served three four-year terms (12 years total) before being term-limited. Later elected Riverhead Town Clerk in 2023.
sources: Riverhead News-Review, “Town Board members thank Wooten for his 12 years of service” (Dec. 2019).
RiverheadLOCAL, “Former councilman James Wooten returns to Riverhead Town Hall” (Dec. 3, 2020).

councilMembers.name: George Gabrielsen
councilMembers.note: Elected in a November 2009 special election to fill the remaining term of a departed councilman, then won a full four-year term in 2011. Did not seek re-election in 2015, citing business commitments.
sources: Riverhead News-Review, “Gabrielsen will not seek re-election, cites business commitments” (April 2015).

councilMembers.name: Jodi Giglio
councilMembers.note: Elected in 2009 and re-elected in 2013, serving about a decade on the Town Board before resigning to take office in the NY State Assembly (District 2), sworn in January 5, 2021.
sources: RiverheadLOCAL, “Jodi Giglio sworn in as member of State Assembly” (Jan. 6, 2021).
New York State Assembly, Jodi Giglio member biography.

councilMembers.name: Tim Hubbard
councilMembers.note: Elected Town Supervisor in November 2023, taking office January 2024 (see Supervisors above).
sources: Riverhead News-Review, “Hulse, Hubbard sworn into office in town ceremony” (Jan. 2016).

councilMembers.name: Catherine Kent
councilMembers.note: A retired teacher, elected councilwoman in November 2017 by a narrow margin over Frank Beyrodt. Served one term, then unsuccessfully challenged incumbent Yvette Aguiar for Town Supervisor in November 2021.
sources: Riverhead News-Review, “Live Riverhead town election results” (Nov. 2017) — Kent narrowly edges Beyrodt for the second council seat.
Riverhead News-Review, “Kent to oppose Aguiar for Town Supervisor” (Feb. 2021).

councilMembers.name: Frank Beyrodt
councilMembers.note: Lost a close race for council in 2017, then won a seat in November 2019. Served one term and did not seek re-election in 2023.
sources: Riverhead News-Review, “Endorsement: Frank Beyrodt, Diane Tucci for Town Board” (Nov. 2019).
RiverheadLOCAL, “Board members, staff and residents bid farewell to three elected officials at final Town Board meeting of 2023” (Dec. 2023).

councilMembers.name: Kenneth Rothwell
councilMembers.note: Appointed in January 2021 to fill the vacancy left by Jodi Giglio's move to the Assembly, then won his first full term in November 2021. Currently serving.
sources: RiverheadLOCAL, “Meet Riverhead Town's new councilman, Kenneth Rothwell” (Jan. 2021).

councilMembers.name: Robert "Bob" Kern
sources: Riverhead News-Review / Patch candidate profiles — Bob Kern (2021, 2025).

councilMembers.name: Joann Waski
sources: Riverhead News-Review, “Election 2023: Meet the candidates for Town Council” (Oct. 2023).

councilMembers.name: Denise Merrifield
sources: Riverhead News-Review, “Election 2023: Meet the candidates for Town Council” (Oct. 2023).
